referenced_message.content:
Casual play is why normal, hard, challenging and heroic difficultys exist. You're asking the most difficult content in the game to be trivial for casual players, when trivial, non-competetive content already exists for you.

With a mid game build, using the recommended weapon archetypes for the class, heroic enemies take multiple mags to down. The damage just isn't there, and that's the difficulty that should be where players with synergised builds (set bonuses, correct talents and purple mods etc) should be farming for the best rolls on their T1 gear, ready to take on legendary.

You do realise every game ever strives for balance right?

Shotguns will be nerfed, that's a fact. There is no dev in the world that would let something that sits that far above everything else in the game slide. That's obvious.

Now it's up to us to help the devs with suggestions on what to do about it. Because if they just nerf shotgun damage into the ground as a fix, vanguard becomes a class that just isn't viable to play. That's what we don't want.

So instead of saying noooo don't nerf my build devs! We are better off striving for actual balance that leaves us even better gameplay, aren't we?

referenced_message.content:
That being said, Commando desperately needs a buff to its permanent passive weapon damage and/or crit stats. 

On any non-shotgun setup, using the class's intended archetypes (AR and SMG) feels completely underwhelming for a dedicated glass-cannon spec. If you patch the broken shotgun pellet loop without raising the baseline passive scaling for full-auto weapons, you aren't balancing the class, you are completely killing its mid-game progression curve. 

And before the counter-argument is made that Heroic shouldn't be trivial, this isn't about making it easy. It's about making it reasonably farmable for players who have a structurally correct build (proper gear sets, coherent mod synergy) but lack the optimized stat rolls. 

Heroic is the exact tier where you are supposed to farm for those max rolls. If a dedicated DPS class running its intended weapons requires near-perfect stats just to avoid tedious, multi-mag clear times on the primary farm difficulty, the progression loop is fundamentally broken. Legendary is the benchmark for optimization; Heroic needs to be viable for progression, and without broken shotguns, commando just isn't doing that right now.

referenced_message.author: rixelated
referenced_message.content:
So first of all, I just realised I must have made a typo and put 9 instead of 8 pellets, or calculated it with my multi shot chance (15.66%), either way it ends on 99.77%.

That being said, heres the breakdown of how it works out, and the real bullet return chance:

This is all based on 8 pellets being counted as seperate vectors, which is what is happening now.

Concerted strike: 88.87%

+ ammo refill: 99.28% (requires kill or assist in the first 6s of tac link, and every 6s thereafter)

+ optimised armor: 99.52% (requires smart cover)

Then we have field supplies, I have fully tested this tbh, looking at the numbers either it does nothing at all for a 2 round mag, or it rounds up to 1, which is broken asf 😅 

Either way, that's how you get 99.52% (rather than 99.77% which was wrong, but is so close that it makes no actual difference in practice)

and that doesnt even count misdirection insurance.... Which gives the same bonus per pellet missed, since pellets count as individual bullets.

So outside of tac link, in that window of "vulnerability" everyone seems to think exists.... with concerted strike and misdirection insurance you're looking at a floor of 88.87% chance of a bullet return (that's concerted strike proccing 8x, once per pellet)

The more pellets miss due to spread, the higher the chance of bullet return goes. concerted strike is on hit, misdirection insurance is on miss.

One other thing to note is, you might be having a problem with jitter, packet loss, server tick rate etc. any of those can force shot that don't register, and therefore don't proc the needed talents. most people recommend the KSG if that is the case due to higher mag size. which also highlights the broken synergy between double barrel and vanguard commando, if double barrel itself gets nerfed, everyone will simply switch shotguns 🤷‍♂️

Cool, so SMG's and DPS then. Let's see what the math says using the first wave vector, the example you used......

refund chance:
vector - 48.63%
double barrel - 99.77%
so effective firing time during tac link for DB is 99.9% (assuming no interuptions in connection or target)
That means effective mag capacity of the vector become 48.66 bullets, at 1200RPM it MUST reload every 2.43s

That means if we deduct reload time, calculate bullets fired in the 19s tac link window, and multiply that by max possible base dmg per shot, we get:

First wave vector: 4,030 DPS
Double barrel shotgun: 15,717 DPS

So... during that 19s tac link window, the Double barrel currently: 

```diff
- Deals 215% more single shot damage than a dedicated sniper rifle, at 4x the speed, in full auto, with a bottomless mag, and achieves 290%, or 3.9x more DPS than the highest DPS SMG in the game
```

So I'm not really sure where you're saying the balance is here. Can't argue with the math. DB is currently outperforming every gun in the game, in all areas due to its interaction with tac link, and its pellet vector multiplier.

referenced_message.author: chewybarka
referenced_message.content:
Vanguard needs changes, but I do not believe the class itself is the core issue.
The reason Vanguard is currently so dominant is because it benefits heavily from a system that allows players to stack certain attributes to extreme levels. As discussed regarding Defense Reduction, the real problem is the lack of meaningful soft caps and hard caps across major combat statistics.
If Vanguard is simply nerfed directly, another build will eventually take its place because the underlying issue remains unchanged. We've seen this happen in many games where one dominant build is replaced by the next dominant build after balance changes.
Instead, I believe the game should introduce soft caps and hard caps on key attributes such as:
Weapon Damage
Critical Chance
Critical Damage
Multishot Chance
Other high-impact combat statistics
For example, once a player reaches a soft cap, additional investment would provide diminishing returns. A hard cap would then prevent any attribute from reaching unhealthy levels.
This approach would naturally pull Vanguard back in line without making the class feel weak or unplayable. At the same time, it would also reduce excessive damage stacking, and other balance extremes throughout the game.
The goal should not be to punish players for choosing Vanguard. The goal should be to ensure that no build can push a single attribute so far that it becomes significantly stronger than everything else.
By implementing soft caps and hard caps across the stat system, Vanguard, damage builds, skill builds, and future builds would all exist within a healthier balance range. This creates more build diversity, more meaningful choices, and a more stable balance environment for future content updates.

referenced_message.author: agent.nene
referenced_message.content:
There's a common misconception that simply equipping a Double Barrel (DB) and switching to Vanguard instantly makes you overpowered. But let's put that in context.
Have you actually played Elite with random players — not your usual speedrunning crew? The reality is that the vast majority of those players, regardless of their builds, are running highly inefficient setups. So yes, a Vanguard DB build will absolutely demolish those players in DPS — but that's not exactly a high bar.

The real test is matching up against another Vanguard DB player who has actually invested time into properly min/maxing their build. In that scenario, the difference is night and day — we're talking 200 million DPS versus 30 million. The build alone means nothing without the optimization behind it.
